text: The nearest clinic is two bus transfers away and I can't get there without taking a full day off work.
I skipped the follow-up because I couldn't afford another $200 visit.
My doctor never listens, so I've started ignoring the advice I'm given.
The first available appointment was four months out.
There's no specialist within a hundred miles of me, so I just go without.
Even with insurance, the bills piled up and I'm scared of the debt.
I finally feel heard — my new provider actually explains things and I trust her judgment.
I sat in the waiting room for almost two hours past my scheduled time.
The paperwork was all in English and no one could help me translate it, so I gave up.
They wanted me to pre-pay the whole procedure and I just don't have that kind of money.
I doubt the diagnosis — it felt rushed and dismissive, like they didn't really look at me.
It took three months just to get a callback to schedule the test.
